title: Upgrade, CONNECT, Tunnels, WebSocket Boundaries, and Safe Transitions · Go Build Room
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine for upgrade, connect, tunnels, websocket boundaries, and safe transitions. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.
Parse CONNECT authority-form, validate host and port policy, authenticate proxy use, establish the target, then transition after a successful response.
Transfer connection, buffered unread octets, deadlines, close ownership, and cancellation atomically to the selected protocol or tunnel.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Upgrade Handshake
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 5437 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Upgrade Handshake.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate connection upgrade, upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Upgrade Handshake, keep Upgrade Handshake connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A workshop protocol team handles case 5445 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Continue HTTP/1.1 safely after a rejected transition only when buffered bytes, framing, peer behavior, and parser state remain unambiguous.
